title: senecio obovatus var. elliottii
intro: Senecio obovatus var. elliottii, also known as Elliott's Jacob's Ladder, is a plant species within the daisy family that is native to the southeastern United States. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can aid in relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including reducing fever and promoting digestion, due to its saponin and flavonoid content. Traditionally, Senecio obovatus var. elliottii has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
